JER.17.26.definition: to go or come (in a wide variety of applications)
a city (a place guarded by waking or a watch) in the widest sense (even of a mere encampment or post)
jehudah (or judah), the name of five israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory
(as noun) a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly (as adverb, with or without preposition) around
jerushalaim or jerushalem, the capital city of palestine
the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
binjamin, youngest son of jacob; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory
properly, a part of; hence (prepositionally), from or out of in many senses
lowland, i.e., (with the article) the maritime slope of palestine
properly, a part of; hence (prepositionally), from or out of in many senses
a mountain or range of hills (sometimes used figuratively)
properly, a part of; hence (prepositionally), from or out of in many senses
the south (from its drought); specifically, the negeb or southern district of judah, occasionally, egypt (as south to palestine)
to go or come (in a wide variety of applications)
a step or (collectively, stairs, as ascending); usually a holocaust (as going up in smoke)
properly, a slaughter, i.e., the flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice (the victim or the act)
a donation; euphemistically, tribute; specifically a sacrificial offering (usually bloodless and voluntary)
frankincense (from its whiteness or perhaps that of its smoke)
to go or come (in a wide variety of applications)
properly, an extension of the hand, i.e., (by implication) avowal, or (usually) adoration; specifically, a choir of worshippers
a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)
(the) self-existent or eternal; jeho-vah, jewish national name of god

JER.17.27.definition: used very widely as demonstrative, lo!; interrogative, whether?; or conditional, if, although; also oh that!, when; hence, as a negative, not
not (the simple or abs. negation); by implication, no; often used with other particles

JER.17.27.gloss: But if ye will not hearken
JER.17.27.definition: to hear intelligently (often with implication of attention, obedience, etc.; causatively, to tell, etc.)
near, with or among; often in general, to
to be (causatively, make, pronounce or observe as) clean (ceremonially or morally)
properly, self (but generally used to point out more definitely the object of a verb or preposition, even or namely)
a day (as the warm hours), whether literal (from sunrise to sunset, or from one sunset to the next), or figurative (a space of time defined by an asso
intermission, i.e (specifically) the sabbath
properly, a failure of, i.e., (used only as a negative particle, usually with a prepositional prefix) not, except, without, unless, besides, because n
to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absolute and relative
a burden; figuratively, an utterance, chiefly a doom, especially singing; mental, desire

an opening, i.e., door or gate

intermission, i.e (specifically) the sabbath
to burn or set on fire; figuratively, to desolate
fire (literally or figuratively)
an opening, i.e., door or gate
to eat (literally or figuratively)
a citadel (from its height)
jerushalaim or jerushalem, the capital city of palestine

JER.17.27.gloss: and it shall not be quenched
JER.17.27.definition: to expire or (causatively) to extinguish (fire, light, anger)
